rules.description: All fallible operations in core/ must return Result<T, E>. Store mutations return Result<T, LayoutError | ValidationError>. Storage operations return Promise<Result<T, StorageError>>. Use domain-specific error constructors from @/core/result/constructors.

Branded ID types (BinId, LayerId, CategoryId, LayoutId, DesignId) must be used in all function signatures. Brand constructors (binId(), layerId()) applied at deserialization boundaries. Never pass bare strings between functions.

Stores must use create<State>()(immer((set, get) => { ... })) pattern. Mutations that can fail return Result<T, E>. Always-succeed mutations call set() with no return. Queries return bare values. Use get() for reading state in closures.

External code must import from @/core/storage (barrel). Internal modules (LayoutService, LayoutManager, backends/) are implementation details. Atomic operations (saveLayoutWithMetadata, etc.) are preferred over legacy CRUD.
All limit validations must reference the CONSTRAINTS object. No magic numbers. Grid: 0.5-50, layers: 1-10, categories: 1-20, label: 24 chars, name: 64 chars, notes: 256 chars, layouts: 500.

Create errors via constructors (validationOutOfBounds(), layoutLayerLimit(), etc.) from @/core/result/constructors. Never create error objects manually. Use unknownError() to wrap unexpected exceptions.
